weekTitle: Sample Week: Government Power in Action
weekSubtitle: Replace this file each week with your AP Government stories.

days.title: Congress Questions an Executive Branch Decision
days.summary: Lawmakers are questioning how an executive agency carried out a recent policy. Supporters argue the agency is doing its job by implementing the law. Critics argue Congress needs stronger oversight. This story connects to separation of powers because each branch has a different role, and it connects to checks and balances because Congress can investigate executive branch action.
questions: What AP Government concept best fits this story? Explain using one fact.
Which institution has power in this story, and what kind of power is it using?
Write a claim-evidence-reasoning response that connects this event to checks and balances.

days.title: Voters React to a New Public Policy Proposal
days.summary: A new policy proposal is drawing reactions from voters, interest groups, and elected officials. Supporters say the policy solves a public problem. Opponents say it creates new costs or limits freedom. This story connects to public opinion because elected officials often respond to what citizens value, fear, or demand.
questions: What groups or citizens are trying to influence government?
How might public opinion shape what elected officials do next?
Connect this story to representation in a democracy.

days.title: State Leaders Respond to a National Policy Debate
days.summary: State leaders are responding differently to a national policy debate. Some states support the federal approach, while others want to create their own rules. This story connects to federalism because power is divided between national and state governments.
questions: How does this story show federalism?
Why might states respond differently to the same national issue?
Write a CER paragraph connecting the story to federalism.

days.title: A Court Case Raises Questions About Individual Rights
days.summary: A court is reviewing a disagreement involving individual rights and government power. One side argues the government rule is necessary. The other side argues the rule violates constitutional protections. This story connects to judicial review because courts can decide whether government action follows the Constitution.
questions: What right or liberty is at issue?
How does the judicial branch check government power?
Connect this story to constitutional limits on government.

days.title: Citizens Organize Around a Local or National Issue
days.summary: Citizens are organizing to influence a government decision. They may contact elected officials, attend meetings, speak publicly, vote, or join groups. This story connects to participatory democracy because citizens can shape public policy beyond election day.
questions: What form of political participation appears in this story?
Connect this story to participatory democracy.
